rooms.title: The Luxury Suite
rooms.subtitle: Bespoke furnishings and dedicated butler service.
rooms.description: Our Luxury Suite is more than a residence; it is a preserved moment of architectural heritage. Featuring original crown moldings restored by hand and herringbone oak floors, the suite balances historic gravity with contemporary technical precision. The separate living salon offers a private library of local history and a curated bar of vintage spirits.
rooms.longDescription: The sleeping quarters are anchored by a bespoke king-size bed draped in 800-thread-count Egyptian cotton, situated beneath a hand-applied gold leaf ceiling detail that catches the first light of dawn over the archives.
rooms.quote: A silent testament to the grandeur of the 19th century, curated for the modern collector.
rooms.archivistNote: This suite once served as the private study of Lord Harrison. The original inkwell stains are still visible on the secretary desk, preserved under crystalline glass.

rooms.title: City Scenery Views
rooms.subtitle: Panoramic horizons over the heritage district.
rooms.description: Floor-to-ceiling windows frame the city's most iconic landmarks, from the spires of St. Martin's Cathedral to the modern skyline beyond. The room features a writing desk positioned to capture the city's changing moods.
rooms.longDescription: Appointed with hand-selected antiques and a curated collection of city maps from the 18th century, this room is a cartographer's dream brought to life in the heart of the heritage district.
rooms.quote: Every window is a canvas; every view, a masterpiece painted by time.
rooms.archivistNote: The telescope by the north window was formerly owned by the city's chief astronomer and offers views of the same skyline recorded in his 1847 journal.

rooms.title: Private Balcony
rooms.subtitle: Quiet moments overlooking our botanical gardens.
rooms.description: Step through restored French doors onto your own private terrace, overlooking three acres of manicured botanical gardens that have been tended since 1912. The balcony features hand-wrought ironwork and a bistro setting for two.
rooms.longDescription: Inside, the room combines the serenity of the gardens with refined period furnishings, including a four-poster bed dressed in Italian linens and a clawfoot bathtub positioned beside a window of trailing wisteria.
rooms.quote: Where architecture ends, the garden begins — and both were designed by the same dreaming hand.
rooms.archivistNote: The wisteria growing along this balcony was planted by the hotel's founder in 1912, and has bloomed every spring without fail for over a century.

testimonials.text: Staying at the Heritage Archive felt like being a character in a classic novel. Every corner of the hotel is a curated masterpiece. The service was invisible yet omnipresent.
testimonials.author: Eleanor Whitworth
testimonials.title: London, UK

testimonials.text: The attention to historical detail is unmatched. From the original gas lamp fittings to the hand-restored frescoes, every element tells a century-old story.
testimonials.author: James Cavendish
testimonials.title: New York, USA

testimonials.text: A sanctuary of calm in the heart of the city. The botanical gardens alone are worth the visit, but the suites are simply otherworldly.
testimonials.author: Arabella Fontaine
testimonials.title: Paris, France

facilities.title: Fine Dining
facilities.subtitle: A journey through forgotten recipes and modern culinary mastery, led by Michelin-starred excellence.

facilities.title: Azure Sanctuary
facilities.subtitle: Our gilded spa offers bespoke treatments inspired by ancient wellness traditions, in a setting of marble and tranquility.
